title: Air-fryer salmon
subtitle: Garlic and lemon salmon fillets
excerpt: Salmon fillets seasoned with garlic, lemon, salt, and a little oil, cooked in the air fryer in under 12 minutes. The flesh stays moist at the centre while the surface sets firm. A very fast weeknight dinner. Serves four.
sourceNotes: Salmon fillets are among the most reliably successful air-fryer dishes. The moderate oil content of salmon means the fish self-bastes during cooking, and the short cooking time at high heat prevents the albumin (the white protein) from seizing and drying out the surface. Standard technique used by home cooks and documented in air-fryer recipe guides from 2019 onwards.

content.text: Salmon is forgiving in the air fryer. The oil in the fish does most of the work, and because the cooking time is so short, there is little window for the fish to go wrong. The main thing to watch is the white albumin: if it is oozing aggressively from the surface, the heat is too high or the fish is overdone. Aim for the flesh to be just opaque all the way through, with a very slight translucency at the thickest point if you prefer it medium.

items.prepNote: zest only, plus wedges to serve

content.text: Pat the salmon fillets dry. Mix together the grated {{garlic}}, the lemon zest, {{olive-oil}} of olive oil, {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt, and {{black-pepper}} of black pepper. Rub the mixture over the flesh side of each fillet. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 3 minutes. Place the fillets skin-side down in the basket. Cook for 9–11 minutes until the flesh is just opaque throughout. Serve immediately with lemon wedges.

items.symptom: White albumin oozing from the surface
items.fix: Reduce to 190°C and check at 8 minutes. The albumin is still edible but signals the fish is past medium. Pull the salmon as soon as the flesh is just opaque.

items.cause: Basket not lightly oiled, or skin very thin.
items.fix: Brush a light film of oil on the basket before cooking, or line with perforated air fryer paper.

items.cause: Overcooked or the fillet was too thin (under 2.5 cm at the thickest).
items.fix: Thin tail-end fillets cook in 7–8 minutes. Check early and pull before they tighten.

content.text: Well-done salmon is opaque throughout, firm, and flakes easily. Medium salmon has an opaque exterior and a slightly translucent, custard-like centre that holds its shape when pressed. Both are safe to eat from sustainably farmed Atlantic salmon bought from a reliable fishmonger or supermarket. Medium is better.
